title: Transfer data to S3 using Amazon Kinesis Firehose

tasks.title: Task 1: Sign in to the AWS Management Console

steps.text: Click on the Open Console button, and you will get redirected to AWS Console in a new browser tab.

On the AWS sign-in page, Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12-digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab. Now copy your User Name and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in the AWS Console and click on the Sign in button
On the AWS sign-in page,
Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12-digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab.
Now copy your User Name and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in the AWS Console and click on the Sign in button
Once Signed In to the AWS Management Console, Make the default AWS Region as US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1.
Note: If you face any issues, please go through FAQs and Troubleshooting for Labs.?
Make sure you are in the US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1 Region.
Navigate to S3 by clicking on the Services menu at the top, then click on S3 in the Storage section.
On the S3 Page, click on Create Bucket and fill in the bucket details. Bucket type : General purpose Bucket name: Enter kinesisfirehose-lab Note: You can enter any unique name of your choice.
On the S3 Page, click on Create Bucket and fill in the bucket details.
Bucket type : General purpose
Bucket name: Enter kinesisfirehose-lab Note: You can enter any unique name of your choice.
Bucket name: Enter kinesisfirehose-lab
Note: You can enter any unique name of your choice.
Task 2: Create an S3 Bucket
Object ownership: Select the ACLs disabled (recommended) option
Uncheck the option, Block all public access, and check the acknowledge option.
Task 2: Create an S3 Bucket
Leave other settings as default.
Click on Create Bucket.
Task 2: Create an S3 Bucket
Select the Created S3 bucket and click on Copy ARN button to copy the s3 bucket arn and note it down in notepad for later use
Task 2: Create an S3 Bucket

tasks.title: Task 3: Create an Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose delivery stream

steps.text: Navigate to Kinesis by clicking on the Services menu at the top, then click on Kinesis in the Analytics section.
On the Get started page, Select amazon Data Firehose and click on the Create Firehose stream button.
Under Choose source and destination section: Source: Select Direct PUT Destination: Select Amazon S3
Under Choose source and destination section:
Source: Select Direct PUT
Destination: Select Amazon S3
Under the Firehose stream name section: Firehose stream name: Enter whiz-demo
Firehose stream name: Enter whiz-demo
Task 3: Create an Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose delivery stream
Under Transformand convert records - optional: Do nothing keep everything as disabled
Under Transformand convert records - optional:
Do nothing keep everything as disabled

Under Destination settings: S3 bucket: Click on browse and Choose the bucket we have created earlier. Leave the rest as default. Click on Buffer hints, compression and encryption to expand, Under S3 buffer hints: Buffer size: 1 MiB Buffer interval: 60 seconds Note: If you enter buffer size as more than 1 MiB and Buffer interval as more than 60 seconds, then it will take more time to load the sample data in S3.
Under Destination settings:
S3 bucket: Click on browse and Choose the bucket we have created earlier.
Leave the rest as default.
Task 3: Create an Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose delivery stream

Click on Buffer hints, compression and encryption to expand, Under S3 buffer hints: Buffer size: 1 MiB Buffer interval: 60 seconds Note: If you enter buffer size as more than 1 MiB and Buffer interval as more than 60 seconds, then it will take more time to load the sample data in S3.
Click on Buffer hints, compression and encryption to expand, Under S3 buffer hints:
Buffer size: 1 MiB
Buffer interval: 60 seconds
Note: If you enter buffer size as more than 1 MiB and Buffer interval as more than 60 seconds, then it will take more time to load the sample data in S3.

Under Advanced settings: Server-side encryption: Uncheck Enable server-side encryption for source records in the delivery stream. Amazon CloudWatch error logging: Not enabled Service access: Choose existing IAM roleFrom the drop down select role name starts with : KinesisFirehosePolicy-Random-Number Under Tags: Key: Name Value: whiz-stream
Under Advanced settings:
Server-side encryption: Uncheck Enable server-side encryption for source records in the delivery stream.
Amazon CloudWatch error logging: Not enabled
Task 3: Create an Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose delivery stream
Service access: Choose existing IAM roleFrom the drop down select role name starts with : KinesisFirehosePolicy-Random-Number
Service access: Choose existing IAM role
From the drop down select role name starts with : KinesisFirehosePolicy-Random-Number
Under Tags: Key: Name Value: whiz-stream
Under Tags:
Key: Name
Value: whiz-stream
Click on Create Firehose stream
Task 3: Create an Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose delivery stream
Creation of the delivery stream will take a few minutes.
Once created, the Status will be shown as Active.
Task 3: Create an Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose delivery stream
Navigate to the Services menu at the top and choose VPC under Networking and Content Delivery.
Click on Your VPC’s in the left side panel then click on Create VPC
Select VPC Only. Enter the Name tag: MyVPC and enter IPv4 CIDR block: 10.1.0.0/16.
Select VPC Only.
Enter the Name tag: MyVPC and enter IPv4 CIDR block: 10.1.0.0/16.
Task 4: Create a VPC
Then leave the other fields as default and click on Create VPC.
Once created it will be shown like this.
Task 4: Create a VPC
Select Internet Gateways in the left side panel and click on the Create Internet Gateway
Enter the name MyInternetGateway and Click on Create Internet Gateway.
Task 5: Creating an Internet Gateway
Once created, attach it to MyVPC by clicking on Actions at the top and selecting Attach to VPC, and then selecting MyVPC.
Task 5: Creating an Internet Gateway
Select Route Tables on the left side and click on MyVPC route table. Click on Routes and select Edit routes.
Task 5: Creating an Internet Gateway
Select Add Routes then enter 0.0.0.0/0. Set the Target as “Internet Gateway” and choose Your Internet Gateway from the drop-down list. Click on Save changes.
Task 5: Creating an Internet Gateway
Click on Subnets in the left side panel and click on Create Subnet. Select “MyVPC” in VPCID.
Task 6: Create a Subnet
Give whizsub as the name of your subnet and us-east-1a for the availability zone. Enter the IPv4 CIDR Block as 10.1.1.0/24 and click on the Create subnet button.
Make sure you are in the US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1 Region.
Navigate to VPC by clicking on Services at the top of the AWS Console.
Click on VPC (under the Networking & Content Delivery section) or you can also search for VPC.
Click on Your VPCs from the left menu.
Navigate to the VPC you just created, click on Actions then select Create Flow Log.
Enter the name as MyVPCFlowLog
Select Accept in Filter options and then set the “Maximum Aggregation Interval” to 1 minute.
Select Send to Amazon S3 bucket as the Destination.
Enter the ARN of your S3 Bucket, which you've copied earlier, then click on the Create Flow Log button.
Once the flow logs are created, head back to the AWS VPC service, select it, and click on the Flow Logs tab.
Task 7: Create VPC Flow Logs
Now you have successfully learned how to create the VPC Flow Logs.
Make sure you are in the US-East (N.Virginia) us-east-1 region.
Navigate to Services at the top and click on EC2 under Compute.
Select Instances in the left side panel and click on Launch Instances
Enter Name as labec2instance
Select Amazon Linux 2023 kernel 6.1 AMI from the dropdown.
Task 8: Creating an EC2 Instance
Choose an Instance Type: Choose t2.micro.
Task 8: Creating an EC2 Instance
For Key pair: Select Create a new key pair Key pair name: MySSHKey Key pair type: RSA Private key file format: .pem
For Key pair: Select Create a new key pair
Key pair name: MySSHKey
Key pair type: RSA
Private key file format: .pem

In Network Settings Click on Edit Button: VPC: Choose MyVPC Auto-assign public IP: Enable Select Create new Security group Security group name: Enter MyEC2Server_SG Description: Enter Security Group to allow traffic to EC2 To add SSH, Choose Type: SSH Source: Select Anywhere For HTTP, Select the Add Security rule Button Choose Type: HTTP Source: Select Anywhere For HTTPs, Select the Add Security rule Button Choose Type: HTTPS Source: Select Anywhere
In Network Settings Click on Edit Button:
VPC: Choose MyVPC
Auto-assign public IP: Enable
Select Create new Security group
Security group name: Enter MyEC2Server_SG
Description: Enter Security Group to allow traffic to EC2
To add SSH, Choose Type: SSH Source: Select Anywhere
To add SSH,
Choose Type: SSH
Source: Select Anywhere
For HTTP, Select the Add Security rule Button Choose Type: HTTP Source: Select Anywhere
For HTTP, Select the Add Security rule Button
Choose Type: HTTP
Source: Select Anywhere
For HTTPs, Select the Add Security rule Button Choose Type: HTTPS Source: Select Anywhere
For HTTPs, Select the Add Security rule Button
Choose Type: HTTPS
Source: Select Anywhere
Task 8: Creating an EC2 Instance
Leave others as default, and click on the Launch Instance button.
After 1-5 minutes, the Instance State will change to running, as shown below. Then click on Connect.
Task 8: Creating an EC2 Instance
Under EC2 Instance connect, click on Connect. Here we need to SSH into the instance to generate traffic. You can follow the steps mentioned in SSH into EC2 Instance.
Task 9: Generating Traffic
Once you SSH into the instance, install an Apache Server. To install it, follow the below steps. Run these commands one by one.
Task 9: Generating Traffic
Copy your instance Public IP, open a new tab and paste it into the address bar, and hit enter. It should be shown as below:
Task 9: Generating Traffic

tasks.title: Task 10: Check the logs in S3 Bucket.

steps.text: Now Navigate to S3, Wait for few minutes and refresh the page, and click on Created bucket. You will see the AWSLogs appear under Objects.
Click on AWSLogs -> Account ID -> vpcflowlogs -> us-east-1 -> Year -> Month -> Date.
You will be able to view all the details i.e. the traffic that is being directed to our EC2 Instance.
Once the lab steps are completed, please click the Validation button on the left side panel.
This will validate the resources in the AWS account and display whether you have completed this lab successfully or not.
Sample output :
Task 11: Validation of the Lab
You have successfully created an S3 bucket.
You have successfully created a Kinesis firehose delivery stream.
You have successfully transferred the sample data generated by the EC2 Instance, from VPC Flow Logs to Amazon S3 Bucket using Kinesis firehose delivery stream.
Sign out of AWS Account.
You have completed the lab.
Once you have completed the steps, click on End Lab from the lab console.
